title: Smart Rainwater Harvesting and Quality Monitor
description: A building-mounted civil infrastructure system that automates rooftop rainwater collection by diverting the contaminated first-flush to waste, then continuously monitoring turbidity, pH, and tank fill level before admitting water to a storage cistern. An ESP32 node publishes telemetry over MQTT to a local dashboard and autonomously controls the diversion solenoid valve. Unlike bench-top portable quality monitors, this system is permanently installed in an outdoor weatherproof enclosure and focuses on storage admission decisions rather than point-of-use testing.
objective: Automatically harvest and quality-gate rooftop rainwater by measuring turbidity (<5 NTU) and pH (6.5–8.5) before opening the solenoid diversion valve to the cistern; monitor tank fill percentage via ultrasonic sensing; publish all metrics via MQTT every 30 seconds.
constraints: 5–12 V DC supply (solar + LiFePO4 battery); IP65 outdoor enclosure; Arduino/ESP32 platform; BOM under $80; no mains AC; low-voltage solenoid valve (12 V).
supporting_fact_titles: Water Quality Multi-Parameter Sensing (pH, TDS, DO)
Solenoid Valve for Pneumatic and Fluid Control

element_usage_notes.Turbidity NTU Optical Sensor Module: Installed in a sealed inline tee on the downpipe after the leaf filter. Analog voltage output wired to ESP32 ADC pin 35; NTU calculated via quadratic calibration curve.
element_usage_notes.HC-SR04 Ultrasonic Tank Level Sensor: Mounted at tank lid centre, facing downward. TRIG on GPIO 5, ECHO on GPIO 18 via voltage divider. Fill % updated each measurement cycle.
element_usage_notes.Solenoid Diversion Valve Module: MOSFET gate driven from GPIO 26. Valve energised (flow to cistern) only when pH and turbidity thresholds pass and fill < 95%.
element_usage_notes.ESP32 WiFi + MQTT IoT Telemetry: Connects to local AP; publishes JSON payload to topic rainwater/telemetry every 30 s. Broker address configurable at compile time.

element_interactions.description: If NTU > 5.0 the valve control logic keeps the solenoid de-energised, diverting flow to waste drain.
Valve is forced closed when tank fill exceeds 95% to prevent overflow regardless of water quality.
Sensor readings are packed into the MQTT JSON payload for dashboard visualisation and historical logging.
Tank fill percentage included in MQTT telemetry so operators can schedule usage or maintenance remotely.

integration_code:
# Smart Rainwater Harvesting & Quality Monitor
# ESP32 MicroPython — MQTT telemetry + auto valve control

import machine, time, network, ujson
from umqtt.simple import MQTTClient

# ── Pin assignments ──────────────────────────────────────
TRIG      = machine.Pin(5,  machine.Pin.OUT)
ECHO      = machine.Pin(18, machine.Pin.IN)
PH_ADC    = machine.ADC(machine.Pin(34))
TURB_ADC  = machine.ADC(machine.Pin(35))
VALVE     = machine.Pin(26, machine.Pin.OUT)  # MOSFET gate
LED       = machine.Pin(2,  machine.Pin.OUT)

PH_ADC.atten(machine.ADC.ATTN_11DB)
TURB_ADC.atten(machine.ADC.ATTN_11DB)

# ── Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────
TANK_DEPTH_CM = 100
MQTT_BROKER   = "192.168.1.100"
MQTT_TOPIC    = b"rainwater/telemetry"
SSID          = "YourSSID"
WIFI_PASS     = "YourPass"
INTERVAL_S   = 30

# Quality admit thresholds
PH_MIN, PH_MAX = 6.5, 8.5
NTU_MAX        = 5.0
FILL_CUTOFF    = 95.0

# ── WiFi ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
def connect_wifi():
    wlan = network.WLAN(network.STA_IF)
    wlan.active(True)
    wlan.connect(SSID, WIFI_PASS)
    for _ in range(20):
        if wlan.isconnected():
            print("WiFi:", wlan.ifconfig()[0])
            return True
        time.sleep(0.5)
    print("WiFi failed — running offline")
    return False

# ── Sensors ──────────────────────────────────────────────
def read_level_pct():
    TRIG.value(0); time.sleep_us(2)
    TRIG.value(1); time.sleep_us(10); TRIG.value(0)
    timeout = time.ticks_us()
    while ECHO.value() == 0:
        if time.ticks_diff(time.ticks_us(), timeout) > 30000:
            return -1
    t0 = time.ticks_us()
    while ECHO.value() == 1:
        if time.ticks_diff(time.ticks_us(), t0) > 30000:
            return -1
    dist_cm = time.ticks_diff(time.ticks_us(), t0) * 0.0343 / 2
    fill = (1.0 - dist_cm / TANK_DEPTH_CM) * 100.0
    return round(max(0.0, min(100.0, fill)), 1)

def read_ph():
    raw = sum(PH_ADC.read() for _ in range(10)) / 10
    voltage = raw * 3.3 / 4095.0
    ph = 3.5 * voltage          # calibrate slope/offset per probe
    return round(max(0.0, min(14.0, ph)), 2)

def read_turbidity_ntu():
    raw = sum(TURB_ADC.read() for _ in range(10)) / 10
    v = raw * 3.3 / 4095.0
    ntu = max(0.0, -1120.4 * v * v + 5742.3 * v - 4352.9)
    return round(ntu, 1)

# ── Valve logic ──────────────────────────────────────────
def update_valve(ph, ntu, fill):
    admit = (PH_MIN <= ph <= PH_MAX) and (ntu < NTU_MAX) and (fill < FILL_CUTOFF)
    VALVE.value(1 if admit else 0)
    return admit

# ── Main loop ────────────────────────────────────────────
def main():
    online = connect_wifi()
    client = None
    if online:
        client = MQTTClient("rainwater", MQTT_BROKER)
        try:
            client.connect()
        except Exception as e:
            print("MQTT connect error:", e)
            client = None

    while True:
        fill  = read_level_pct()
        ph    = read_ph()
        ntu   = read_turbidity_ntu()
        admit = update_valve(ph, ntu, fill)

        payload = ujson.dumps({
            "fill_pct": fill,
            "ph": ph,
            "turbidity_ntu": ntu,
            "valve_open": admit
        })
        print(payload)

        if client:
            try:
                client.publish(MQTT_TOPIC, payload)
            except Exception as e:
                print("MQTT publish error:", e)

        LED.value(1); time.sleep_ms(100); LED.value(0)
        time.sleep(INTERVAL_S)

main()
